issues.comment: The method `FileHelper.is_image?` does not exist. `FileHelper` defines `is_supported_image?`, `is_inline_image?`, `is_svg?`, etc., but never `is_image?`. This will raise a `NoMethodError` at runtime, causing the entire image downsizing feature to fail. The correct method is `FileHelper.is_supported_image?(filename)`.

Duplicate `def self.downsize` — the first definition (accepting `max_width, max_height` as separate arguments) is silently overwritten by the second definition (accepting a single `dimensions` string). In Ruby, redefining a method silently overwrites the previous definition, so the first `downsize` is dead code and will never execute. The author likely intended to replace the first definition with the second, not keep both. The first definition should have been removed.

Client-side validation hardcodes 10MB (`10 * 1024` KB) for ALL upload types, replacing the old dynamic lookup `Discourse.SiteSettings['max_' + type + '_size_kb']`. This means non-image attachments that admins allow to be larger than 10MB (e.g., `max_attachment_size_kb = 51200`) will be wrongly rejected client-side, and the error message will always show "10240 KB" as the limit regardless of actual server configuration. The server-side downsizing feature only applies to images, but this client-side change affects all file types indiscriminately.
